title: 10 best herbal capsules for pink eye
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for pink eye are a natural remedy designed to alleviate the discomfort and symptoms associated with conjunctivitis, also known as pink eye. These capsules typically contain a blend of herbs such as goldenseal, echinacea, and eyebright, which work together to reduce inflammation, soothe redness, and combat bacterial infections. Examples of herbal capsules that help with pink eye include BioVision's Pink Eye Relief Capsules and Nature's Bounty's Conjunctivitis Formula. By using these herbal capsules, individuals can experience improved vision, reduced swelling, and a faster recovery time, allowing them to return to their daily activities with renewed comfort and confidence.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with pink eye because they contain a unique compound called ursolic acid, which has been shown to have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections. Additionally, the herb's anti-inflammatory effects can reduce swelling and itching associated with pink eye. Furthermore, purple conefflower's antioxidant properties can also help protect the eyes from further damage caused by inflammation and oxidative stress, promoting a faster recovery from the infection.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Echinacea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness associated with pink eye, providing relief from discomfort.

Immunomodulatory effects: The herbs in purple coneflower capsules can help stimulate the immune system to fight off infection-causing pathogens, such as bacteria or viruses, that contribute to pink eye.
Antimicrobial properties: Echinacea contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms known to cause conjunctivitis, helping to prevent the spread of infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and treat pink eye.
Fruits: The fruits contain antioxidants and flavonoids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers in sufficient quantity for your desired dosage.
Dry the flowers in a well-ventilated area away from direct sunlight and moisture within 24 hours.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to ensure uniformity and quality control.
Mix 500 milligrams of powdered echinacea purpurea with 250 milliliters of vegetable glycerin and 10 drops of vitamin e oil in a sterile glass jar.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with pink eye because they are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm the affected area. The capsules contain flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing, thereby reducing redness, swelling, and discharge associated with pink eye. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of pot marigold capsules may also help combat bacterial infections, speeding up the recovery process and preventing complications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with pink eye.
Antimicrobial: The plant's bioactive compounds, including triterpenes and glycosides, exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the bacterial or viral infection causing pink eye.
Soften mucous membranes: Pot marigold has been shown to have a soothing effect on mucous membranes, which can help ease discomfort, reduce discharge, and promote healing in cases of pink eye.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing bioactive compounds, roots help soothe and reduce inflammation in the eyes.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, flowers alleviate symptoms of pink eye by reducing redness and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers and dry them using shade drying method for at least two weeks.
Grind dried calendula flowers into fine powder using coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Sift ground calendula powder through fine-mesh strainer to remove lumps and large particles, then fill a digital scale with exactly 500 milligrams of the powder.
Add 250 milliliters of purified water to the ground calendula powder in a clean glass jar and steep for one hour at room temperature.
Pour the liquid mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filler machine or spoon, then store the finished capsules in an airtight container in refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright capsules helps with pink eye because they contain the herb Euphrasia, which has been traditionally used to treat conjunctivitis. The antibacterial properties of eyebright help combat bacterial infections that cause pink eye, reducing inflammation and redness. Additionally, eyebright's anti-inflammatory properties soothe irritated eyes, providing relief from itchiness, burning, and discharge associated with the condition. By targeting both the infection and inflammation, herbal eyebright capsules offer a natural and effective way to alleviate symptoms of pink eye.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Eyebright capsules contain compounds that reduce swelling and inflammation in the conjunctiva, helping to alleviate redness and discomfort associated with pink eye.

Antimicrobial properties: The herb Eyebright (Euphrasia officinalis) has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various bacterial and viral strains that can cause pink eye, such as Haemophilus influenzae and adenovirus, helping to combat infection.

Eye-soothing and lubricating effects: Eyebright capsules contain mucilages that help to soothe and moisturize the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by pink eye, while also providing a protective barrier against further irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the eyebright plant are used to make medicinal capsules for pink eye due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and reduce redness in the affected eyes.

Buds: Eyebright buds are used to create capsules for pink eye treatment as they contain high concentrations of flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities, helping to combat infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a low-temperature drying method for two days.
Grind 20 grams of dried euphrasia officinalis into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 100mg of silica gel and fill into #00 capsules for a total of 500 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal capsules helps with pink eye because they contain berberine, a natural compound that has potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. When taken orally, berberine can help to reduce inflammation and kill the bacteria that cause pink eye, also known as conjunctivitis. By targeting the root cause of the infection, goldenseal capsules can promote faster healing and alleviate symptoms such as redness, discharge, and itchiness. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties can soothe the eyes and provide relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Goldenseal contains berberine, a bioactive compound that has potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to combat bacterial and viral infections causing pink eye.

Berberinoids: Berberinoids are alkaloid compounds found in goldenseal that have been shown to possess antiseptic and antibacterial properties, helping to reduce the severity of pink eye symptoms such as redness, itching, and discharge.

Saponins: Goldenseal contains saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the affected area, thereby alleviating pink eye discomfort and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goldenseal plant are rich in berberine, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections associated with pink eye.
Rhizomes: Goldenseal rhizomes contain a high concentration of alkaloids, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe pink eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried goldenseal root powder from reliable sources and sift it through fine mesh for purity.
Measure out 500 milligrams of dried goldenseal root powder into small glass bowl.
Combine measured goldenseal root powder with 200 milliliters of distilled water in stainless steel pot.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to extract active compounds then filter through cheesecloth.
Fill empty capsules with extracted liquid and seal immediately for long-term storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm capsules helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The mucilage extracted from the inner bark of the slippery elm tree has been used for centuries to soothe irritated eyes and reduce inflammation associated with conjunctivitis, also known as pink eye. By coating and protecting the sensitive tissues in the eyes, slippery elm capsules can help alleviate discomfort, itchiness, and redness, allowing the natural healing process to occur more efficiently.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Slippery elm's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with pink eye, promoting healing and comfort.

Soothing mucilage: The gel-like mucilage in slippery elm helps protect and soothe the eyes by forming a barrier against irritants and bacteria, reducing discomfort and promoting drainage of excess discharge.

Antimicrobial properties: Slippery elm's antimicrobial compounds may help combat bacterial infections that can cause pink eye, such as Haemophilus influenzae or Streptococcus pneumoniae, by inhibiting their growth and replication.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Barks: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and redness associated with pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra bark and clean it thoroughly with lukewarm water and gentle soap solution.
Dry the cleaned ulmus rubra bark in a warm and dry place for at least four days or until it is brittle.
Grind the dried ulmus rubra bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one teaspoon of the ground ulmus rubra powder with 250 milliliters of boiling water to create a decoction that steeps for 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into small capsules filled with 500 milligrams of the herbal mixture per capsule and seal tightly for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with pink eye because they contain a powerful anti-inflammatory agent that soothes and calms the infected eyes. The gel inside the capsule is rich in antioxidants, which help to reduce swelling and redness, promoting quick recovery from this common and contagious condition. Additionally, aloe's antimicrobial properties aid in eliminating bacteria that cause pink eye, allowing the affected area to heal faster and more effectively.
remedy_properties: Soothing anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera's unique combination of fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing quick relief from pink eye symptoms.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Herbal aloe capsules contain antioxidants like vitamin C and E, which help combat free radicals that can exacerbate pink eye, while also inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and viruses that cause the condition.

Moisturizing and lubricating agents: Aloe vera's natural humectants and emollients help soothe and moisturize the eyes, reducing dryness and irritation associated with pink eye, allowing for faster healing and recovery.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in vitamins and antioxidants, aloe leaves help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Roots: Containing bioactive compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, aloe roots aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the aloe vera gel by spreading it thinly on paper towels for 3 days.
Blend 100g of dried aloe vera gel powder with 50g of vitamin c powder in a mixing bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the aloe vera-vitamin c mixture and seal them tightly.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic capsules helps with pink eye because they contain allicin, a potent compound that has antimicrobial properties. When taken orally, allicin is absorbed into the bloodstream and transported to the eyes, where it helps to combat the infection-causing bacteria, such as Haemophilus influenzae and Moraxella catarrhalis. Additionally, garlic's antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce eye redness, swelling, and discharge associated with pink eye, promoting faster recovery and alleviating discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Herbal garlic capsules contain compounds like allicin, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Staphylococcus aureus and Pseudomonas aeruginosa, common causes of pink eye.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Garlic capsules contain compounds that help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which is often a symptom of pink eye caused by bacterial or viral infection.
Antioxidant properties: Herbal garlic capsules are rich in antioxidants like vitamin C and E, which help protect the eyes from oxidative damage caused by free radicals and promote overall eye health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sulfur compounds, garlic roots have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help combat bacterial infections and reduce inflammation associated with pink eye.
Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of allicin, the rhyzome is the primary storage organ of garlic's medicinal compounds, making it an ideal choice for medicinal capsules to treat pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality allium sativum bulbs dry them for at least two weeks and then grind into a fine powder.
Mix one part powdered allium sativum with three parts excipient such as silica or calcium carbonate in a large bowl.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the herbal blend and seal tightly to prevent contamination.
Label and date the capsules clearly indicating ingredients dosages and recommended usage for pink eye treatment.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight and moisture for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with pink eye because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The capsules contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, which work together to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and viral infections that cause conjunctivitis. By soothing the affected area and fighting off the underlying infection, maidenhair tree capsules provide natural relief from pink eye symptoms such as redness, itchiness, and discharge, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory: These properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can alleviate symptoms such as redness, itchiness, and discomfort associated with Pink Eye.

Immunomodulatory: The immunomodulatory effects of Maidenhair Tree capsules can help regulate the body's immune response, reducing the severity of Pink Eye symptoms by inhibiting excessive inflammatory responses.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of these capsules have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that cause Pink Eye, such as Haemophilus influenzae and Streptococcus pneumoniae.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections associated with pink eye.
Barks: Contain tannins and phenolic acids, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective against pink eye pathogens.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves and stems according to organic farming practices within 24 hours of planting.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 7 days or until brittle.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle with 30 grams per batch.
Mix the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 100 mg of vitamin c powder and 50 mg of honey powder in a small bowl for 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture, using a capsule filler machine at room temperature within 1 hour of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with pink eye because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that targets the underlying causes of the condition. The antioxidants in turmeric help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms such as redness, itchiness, and discharge. Additionally, curcumin has antimicrobial properties that can help to combat bacterial or viral infections that may be contributing to pink eye. By taking turmeric capsules regularly, individuals can experience relief from pink eye symptoms and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help reduce swelling and combat infection in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of pink eye.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in turmeric have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial or viral infections causing pink eye, reducing the severity and duration of the condition.

Flavonoids and saponins: The flavonoids and saponins found in turmeric possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, while also providing protection against further inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, also known as the underground stem, contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce inflammation and soothe pink eye symptoms.
Roots: Turmeric roots are rich in antioxidants and have antimicrobial properties, making them effective against bacterial infections that can cause pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric root curcuma longa for proper quantity measurement and drying purposes.
Dry the harvested turmeric roots in a shaded area or using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours.
Grind dried turmeric roots into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of ground turmeric powder with 100mg of ghee or coconut oil to create a uniform blend.
Fill capsules using the blended mixture and seal them in airtight containers for storage and distribution.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with pink eye because they contain natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and reduce inflammation in the affected eyes. The capsanthin in elderberries has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, including those responsible for conjunctivitis, while the flavonoids present in the berries have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate redness, itchiness, and discharge associated with pink eye.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Elderberry's antibacterial compounds, such as anthocyanins, can help combat bacterial infections that cause pink eye, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in elderberry, like quercetin and kaempferol, have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with pink eye.

Antioxidant activity: Elderberry's rich antioxidant content helps protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate pink eye symptoms and promote scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling associated with pink eye.
Barks: The bark of the elder tree contains anthocyanins, which have antioxidant properties that can help combat bacterial infections causing pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh sambucus nigra flowers at dawn when dewy for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours or air dry them in shade for 3 days.
Grind 50g of dried sambucus nigra flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder and sift through a mesh strainer to remove lumps.
Mix the powdered sambucus nigra with 250ml of distilled water and simmer for 30 minutes in a double boiler to create a tea infusion.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 500mg of the cooled tea infusion and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with pink eye because they contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This active ingredient reduces swelling and redness in the affected eye, providing relief from discomfort and itchiness associated with pink eye. Additionally, licorice root has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial or viral infections causing pink eye. Antiviral and antibacterial properties: The active compounds in licorice root, including isothiocyanates and phenolic acids, inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause pink eye, such as Staphylococcus aureus and adenovirus.
Sooothing and demulcent properties: Licorice root's mucilage helps protect and soothe the eyes by forming a protective barrier on the surface of the conjunctiva, reducing irritation and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with pink eye.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dry glycyrrhiza glabra roots in bulk for processing.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mill or coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered root with glycerin and water to form a paste.
Fill gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture and seal.
Store the prepared capsules in an airtight container for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with pink eye because they provide a soothing, anti-inflammatory effect on the affected area. The mucilage in marshmallow root coats and protects the irritated tissues, reducing redness and swelling. Additionally, the antioxidants present in the herb help to reduce oxidative stress and promote healthy tissue repair. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, marshmallow capsules can help alleviate the discomfort and symptoms associated with pink eye, allowing for a faster and more effective recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal marshmallow capsules contain mucilages that help reduce inflammation and soothe red, irritated eyes, providing relief from pink eye symptoms.
Moisturizing properties: The gel-like substance in marshmallow root helps to lubricate and moisturize the eyes, reducing dryness and itchiness associated with pink eye.

Antiviral properties: Marshmallow root has been shown to have antiviral activity, which can help combat the viral infection that causes pink eye (conjunctivitis), promoting recovery and preventing complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Marshmallow roots contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and lubricates the eyes, reducing discomfort associated with pink eye.
Barks: Marshmallow barks have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, making them an effective component for treating pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh althaea officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 12 hours or air dry for 3 days.
Grind the dried althaea officinalis into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of ground althaea officinalis with 250mg of borax in a small bowl to create a 2:1 ratio.
Fill empty capsules with the powder mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with pink eye because they have potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the affected eyes. The menthol in peppermint capsules can also reduce redness and irritation, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving pink eye symptoms such as itchiness, burning, and discharge. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and camphor, reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with pink eye.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents in peppermint capsules, including limonene and beta-pinene, help combat bacterial or viral infections that cause pink eye, preventing them from spreading further.

Cooling and decongestant properties: Peppermint oil's menthol content can help reduce itchiness, burning sensations, and excessive tearing by providing a cooling sensation and reducing swelling in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots are used due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps soothe and calm the symptoms of pink eye.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of peppermint contains compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or store-bought dried peppermint flowers in bulk according to packaging instructions.
Dry the fresh peppermint leaves in a single layer using air circulation for at least 24 hours or up to 48 hours until brittle.
Grind 20-25 grams of dried peppermint flowers into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 250-500 milligrams of powdered peppermint per capsule and seal tightly to prevent contamination.
Store the peppermint capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months or use immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and redness associated with this condition. The capsules contain compounds that have been shown to decrease inflammation in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and promoting healing. Barks: Contain bioactive compounds that exhibit antimicrobial properties, effectively fighting off bacterial infections causing pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest urtica dioica leaves and stems to ensure optimal potency and freshness for drying.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator or warm air circulation method for 24 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix the powdered urtica dioica with a binding agent such as gelatin or potato starch in a ratio of 10:1 and fill capsules with approximately 500 milligrams of the blend.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months while maintaining their potency and freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with pink eye because they contain a compound called anethole, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. Fruits: Containing citric acid, flavonoids, and other nutrients that help to combat infection-causing bacteria and viruses, promoting healing and recovery.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves in the early morning or late afternoon when the essential oils are most concentrated.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for 24-48 hours to reduce moisture content and preserve potency.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to increase surface area and bioavailability.
Mix 1 part powdered foeniculum vulgare with 2 parts carrier medium such as potato starch or silica gel, and fill capsule shells with the mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container away from direct sunlight and moisture for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with pink eye because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and irritation in the affected area. Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix one teaspoon of the powdered salvia officinalis with three teaspoons of pure gelatin and allow to infuse for 30 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the infused gelatin mixture and seal tightly.

Mix the powdered herb with 100g of gelatin capsules in a small bowl to achieve a uniform blend.

remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers are a popular choice for making medicinal capsules for pink eye due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and itching.
Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an antioxidant that has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating bacterial conjunctivitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them according to the traditional method within two weeks of harvest.
Grind 500 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part powdered matricaria chamomilla with three parts carrier material such as silica gel or cornstarch in a large bowl for uniform blending.
Fill empty #0 capsules with 250 milligrams of the herbal blend mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency and freshness.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months from the date of filling to ensure optimal shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats capsules helps with pink eye because of their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The beta-glucans present in oats have been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and discharge associated with conjunctivitis. Additionally, the antioxidants in oats capsules help to calm the eyes and promote healing by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation. By taking these capsules orally, the body absorbs the beneficial compounds that are then transported to the affected area, providing natural relief from pink eye symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Oat extracts have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with pink eye, helping to alleviate discomfort and promote healing.
Antimicrobial properties: Herbal oats contain compounds that have antimicrobial effects, which can help combat bacterial infections and prevent the spread of pink eye.

Soothing and emollient properties: Oat extracts are known for their soothing and moisturizing effects on the skin and mucous membranes, making them effective at calming redness, itching, and discharge associated with pink eye.
remedy_parts: Roots: Oat roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: Oat seeds contain beta-glucans, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in ocular infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest avena sativa seeds and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered avena sativa seeds with distilled water in a ratio of 1:20 for 10 minutes.
Add honey as a natural preservative and anti-inflammatory agent to the mixture in a dosage of 5 grams for every 100 grams of mixture for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the avena sativa-honey mixture using a capsule filler machine for 3 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat pink eye with herbal capsules is to combine Echinacea, Goldenseal, and Garlic supplements. Take 2-3 capsules of Echinacea twice a day to reduce inflammation and boost immunity. Combine this with Goldenseal's antibacterial properties by taking 1 capsule daily. Finally, Garlic capsules provide antiviral benefits; take 1-2 capsules daily. Follow the recommended dosages and consult a healthcare professional for proper guidance.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to pink eye that are treated with herbal capsules are conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and meibomian gland dysfunction. Herbal capsules containing ingredients such as Echinacea, Goldenseal, and Eyebright have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. They may also soothe dryness and itching associated with these conditions. Additionally, herbal supplements rich in omega-3 fatty acids can support eye health and promote healing.
